mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 200) triggers central bank rate hikes that destabilize equity markets, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 200.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS > 100k) and declining unemployment (UNRATE < 4.5%) signals a strong labor market that boosts corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined effect of PAYEMS > 100k and UNRATE < 4.5 fails to produce positive 5d returns in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in residential construction (HOUST < 1400) amid rising consumer spending (RSAFS > 370000) indicates shifting demand patterns that create sectoral rotation opportunities, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of HOUST < 1400 and RSAFS > 370000 fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 30% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained expansion in industrial production (INDPRO > 110) signals robust manufacturing activity that drives corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns despite INDPRO remaining above 110 for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDP growth (GDPC1 < 1.5%) during a housing market downturn (HOUST < 1400) indicates recessionary risks that depress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of GDPC1 < 1.5 and HOUST < 1400 fails to produce negative 20d returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment (UNRATE > 0.5pp increase in 1 month) signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns despite UNRATE rising > 0.5pp in 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained expansion in industrial production (INDPRO > 112) during a housing market recovery (HOUST > 1400) indicates broad-based economic strength that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of INDPRO > 112 and HOUST > 1400 fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 35% of cases.
